jokes.text: That's the literal definition of bailing something out. / There will be no bailouts.
jokes.setup: Mike explains bailouts aren't on the table, then describes exactly what a bailout is
jokes.punchline: Someone points out the obvious contradiction, but Mike doubles down anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic political doublespeak satire - saying one thing while doing the opposite with complete confidence

jokes.text: Well, I hope to fiscal fuck the fed has a trillion dollars stuffed in a mattress somewhere.
jokes.setup: The gravity of the banking crisis requiring massive government intervention
jokes.punchline: 'Fiscal fuck' as profane wordplay and the absurd image of the Fed keeping money in mattresses
jokes.explanation: Creative profanity plus absurd imagery - 'fiscal fuck' is clever wordplay for Ben's character

jokes.text: Go to the ATM Monday and dust will come out.
jokes.setup: Asked what happens if they do nothing about the banking crisis
jokes.punchline: Vivid, absurd image of ATMs literally empty - dispensing dust instead of money
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and striking visual metaphor for economic collapse - highly quotable

jokes.text: Thanks, Kent's autism.
jokes.setup: Kent makes a socially awkward but accurate observation about AIDS
jokes.punchline: Selina dismissively attributes his bluntness to autism rather than engaging with the point
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment showing Selina's callousness, though relies on disability humor

jokes.text: At least when Truman made the decision to drop the bomb, he wasn't fucking anyone in Hiroshima.
jokes.setup: Comparing Selina's difficult banking decision to historic presidential choices
jokes.punchline: Darkly comic escalation to nuclear warfare with crude sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation comparing personal conflict of interest to nuclear warfare - very quotable

jokes.text: That we know of.
jokes.setup: Ben's already outrageous Truman/Hiroshima comparison
jokes.punchline: Mike doubles down by suggesting Truman might have had secret Japanese lovers
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that escalate an already absurd premise to new heights

jokes.text: That wood he's chopping, it's not gonna burn right.
jokes.setup: Asked for feedback on Jonah's political ad
jokes.punchline: Focus group fixates on completely irrelevant technical detail instead of political message
jokes.explanation: Realistic absurdity of focus groups missing the point entirely for mundane concerns

jokes.text: His head is too big for his body. But then sometimes his body is too big for his head. He's the wrong shape.
jokes.setup: Continuing criticism of Jonah's appearance in the ad
jokes.punchline: Contradictory but somehow accurate assessment that he's just fundamentally 'wrong shape'
jokes.explanation: Absurd but specific physical comedy - the logic of 'wrong shape' is perfectly illogical

jokes.text: I was like, 'Run, kid.'
jokes.setup: The only positive feedback was about a child in Jonah's ad
jokes.punchline: Even that positive turned negative - warning the child to flee from Jonah
jokes.explanation: Escalates the focus group's rejection of Jonah to protective instincts toward children

jokes.text: Do you morons really not understand that this is a two-way mirror?
jokes.setup: Jonah watching his devastating focus group feedback
jokes.punchline: His outrage reveals he doesn't understand how focus groups work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah character moment - arrogant and clueless about basic professional procedures

jokes.text: I work in the fucking West Wing, you Pepperidge Farm ad motherfuckers.
jokes.setup: Jonah's continued outrage at the focus group
jokes.punchline: 'Pepperidge Farm ad motherfuckers' as a bizarrely specific insult
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and unexpected insult - 'Pepperidge Farm ad motherfuckers' is memorably absurd

jokes.text: Sit your fucking mom jeans ass down, dude.
jokes.setup: Jonah's profane outburst at the focus group
jokes.punchline: Focus group participant matches his energy with unexpected pushback
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jonah getting owned by regular people he's trying to impress

jokes.text: How is Catherine there and also here?
jokes.setup: Selina confused about Catherine's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Her spatial confusion reveals she pays no attention to her daughter
jokes.explanation: Good character beat about Selina's maternal neglect, economically delivered

jokes.text: Why does it keep doing the turning?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to operate some kind of machine to read news articles
jokes.punchline: Her technological incompetence with simple rotating mechanism
jokes.explanation: Basic tech incompetence humor but fits Selina's character well

jokes.text: We need a Jonah whisperer, except somebody who's gonna, like, yell in his face and call him stupid.
jokes.setup: Discussion of managing Jonah's campaign
jokes.punchline: Subverts the gentle 'whisperer' concept with aggressive opposite approach
jokes.explanation: Smart subversion of the 'horse whisperer' concept - perfect for Jonah's needs

jokes.text: You're just sitting here farting into my couch.
jokes.setup: Selina frustrated with Amy's inaction
jokes.punchline: Crude, specific image of literally just producing gas into furniture
jokes.explanation: Crude but specific visual image that's characteristic of Selina's direct style

jokes.text: It's in Annandale, Mike. No one from DC goes to fucking Annandale.
jokes.setup: Mike offering Korean barbecue with his Groupon
jokes.punchline: Dan's geographical snobbery about Virginia suburbs
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dan's pretentiousness about DC geography

jokes.text: Then what's the point?
jokes.setup: Mike offers massage Groupon, clarifies it doesn't include 'release'
jokes.punchline: Dan's blunt dismissal of massage without sexual service
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character moment - Dan's sleaziness delivered with perfect deadpan

jokes.text: You wouldn't know the smart move if it bent you over and fucked you with a Coke bottle.
jokes.setup: Mike arguing with Dan about the Jonah job opportunity
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly graphic and aggressive metaphor from usually mild Mike
jokes.explanation: Shocking character moment - Mike delivering unexpectedly crude aggression, great contrast

jokes.text: I shouted it into my phone on the Acela Quiet Car.
jokes.setup: Amy confessing she called the president a cunt
jokes.punchline: The specific social transgression of shouting profanity on the quiet car
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail - violating train etiquette while committing political suicide

jokes.text: That's my final solution.
jokes.setup: Selina making her banking decision
jokes.punchline: Accidentally echoing Nazi terminology while making economic policy
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference showing Selina's casual ignorance of loaded language

jokes.text: Some straight up JFK level shit.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing his thoughtful window-gazing pose
jokes.punchline: Absurd self-aggrandizement comparing himself to Kennedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah delusion - comparing basic contemplation to presidential gravitas

jokes.text: They said that we can't use the song 'Working Man' anymore because they find you odious.
jokes.setup: Update on campaign music rights
jokes.punchline: Rush's specific word choice 'odious' for rejecting Jonah
jokes.explanation: Great word choice - 'odious' is perfectly formal and crushing

jokes.text: Copyright shit went out with the Zune.
jokes.setup: Being told he can't use copyrighted music
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical comparison to failed Microsoft device
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah logic - connecting unrelated tech failure to legal concepts

jokes.text: Copyright shit went out with the Zune.
jokes.setup: Jonah dismissing copyright concerns
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison linking copyright law to failed Microsoft device
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and illogical Jonah comparison showing his tech ignorance

jokes.text: And if you listen to me instead of your only two brain cells busy butt-fucking each other somewhere in the vast expanses of your misshapen skull...
jokes.setup: Dan's aggressive coaching approach for Jonah
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor for Jonah's stupidity with vivid sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor combining anatomical accuracy with creative profanity - highly quotable

jokes.text: Holy shit, are you here to beg me for a job?
jokes.setup: Dan arriving to potentially take campaign manager role
jokes.punchline: Jonah's delusional belief that Dan would beg him for anything
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jonah's complete delusion about his status

jokes.text: Your banner makes it look like your name is Jon H. Ryan, so great job, communications department.
jokes.setup: Dan critiquing various campaign failures
jokes.punchline: Specific design failure making candidate's name unclear
jokes.explanation: Good observational detail about campaign incompetence at basic level

jokes.text: How do you look so good with all this stress? You're glowing. You actually look younger. What did you do?
jokes.setup: Selina complimenting Gary's appearance
jokes.punchline: Gary's awkward reaction reveals he's had cosmetic work done
jokes.explanation: Sets up Gary's vanity reveal but the setup itself is only mildly amusing

jokes.text: Oh, my God, he noticed. I have a new facial moisturizer. And a new soap.
jokes.setup: Selina noticing Gary looks different
jokes.punchline: Gary's obvious lies about cosmetic surgery being just skincare products
jokes.explanation: Good Gary character moment showing his vanity and terrible lying skills

jokes.text: Do you want to just have sex? / Yeah. / It's got to be quick, though. I got wheels up at 9:30.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Selina and Charlie after serious talk
jokes.punchline: Immediate transition to casual, time-constrained sex planning
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between romance and logistics - very practical approach to intimacy

jokes.text: Make an appointment to freeze eggs.
jokes.setup: Amy witnessing Selina and Charlie's relationship
jokes.punchline: Her biological clock panic expressed as a reminder note
jokes.explanation: Good character beat about Amy's professional vs. personal life anxiety

jokes.text: Hanukah polls higher in Mecca.
jokes.setup: Describing how unpopular bailing out Charlie's bank would be
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison using religious/geographic impossibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect statistical absurdity - brilliant comparison showing impossible unpopularity

jokes.text: I could lose them both. I mean, at this stage they're purely decorative.
jokes.setup: Asked about choosing between cock and balls
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acceptance of sexual decline with age
jokes.explanation: Dark but relatable aging humor delivered with perfect Ben deadpan

jokes.text: That's Korean for 'I hate you.'
jokes.setup: Sue's dismissive response to Korean barbecue suggestion
jokes.punchline: Kent's literal translation interpretation of cultural dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good Kent character moment - overly literal interpretation of cultural interaction

jokes.text: You know what we call people like you back in my day? Retards.
jokes.setup: Dan's aggressive coaching of Jonah
jokes.punchline: Blunt use of outdated offensive term as motivation
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but relies on offensive language without much craft

jokes.text: She's a retired second grade teacher, for Christ's sake. / Yeah, who sucks and gives too much homework.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining how Jonah is losing to a teacher
jokes.punchline: Jonah's childish complaint about homework from his actual teacher
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jonah still holds elementary grudges against his teacher

jokes.text: It's so much more agreeable than anything that gets dished out in prison.
jokes.setup: Bill recommending corned beef hash to someone
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to prison food as his comparison point
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - casual prison reference while making small talk

jokes.text: I am fructose intolerant, you nitwit.
jokes.setup: Food conversation continuing
jokes.punchline: Aggressive dietary restriction revelation with insult
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Bill's prison-hardened aggression over minor issues

jokes.text: I suppose you need something to nervously shit out.
jokes.setup: Watching Jonah eat before his speech
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate observation about pre-performance anxiety
jokes.explanation: Crude but psychologically accurate observation about stress eating

jokes.text: So, who had 12:50 P.M.?
jokes.setup: Selina changing her mind again about bank bailout
jokes.punchline: Ben revealing staff are betting on timing of Selina's flip-flops
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that staff have turned Selina's indecisiveness into office gambling

jokes.text: It's always good to see the most left-swiped face on Tinder.
jokes.setup: Dan greeting Leon West
jokes.punchline: Modern dating app insult about rejection rates
jokes.explanation: Creative modern insult using specific app mechanics - very quotable

jokes.text: Ah, I just blew the lid off of Cuntgate.
jokes.setup: Leon discovering the story about staffers calling Selina a cunt
jokes.punchline: Adding '-gate' suffix to make it sound like major scandal
jokes.explanation: Media satire about creating scandals with Watergate naming convention

jokes.text: Although you do have the perfect face for it.
jokes.setup: Warning Amy about being the face of 'Cuntgate'
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she literally looks like someone associated with that word
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited but economical insult, though relies on crude appearance humor

jokes.text: I was talking to her about how Wendy paid off my student loans and how I love her for it.
jokes.setup: Mike accused of telling Selina to bail out Charlie's bank
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was actually talking about his own financial relationship
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Mike's personal financial struggles

jokes.text: I guess when he had cancer, the doctors removed his ability to stop doing his kid's babysitter.
jokes.setup: Dan suggesting a joke about local mayor
jokes.punchline: Jonah's version implies sexual misconduct with disturbing cancer reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's terrible judgment but relies on offensive implications without craft

jokes.text: I don't recall that macaroni portrait, Mrs. Sherman.
jokes.setup: His former teacher mentioning a childhood art project
jokes.punchline: Jonah's defensive denial of embarrassing childhood memory
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jonah's embarrassment about childhood

jokes.text: Did you hear that dig about the macaroni portrait? Can you fucking believe that?
jokes.setup: Mrs. Sherman's innocent childhood memory
jokes.punchline: Jonah interpreting wholesome teacher memory as political attack
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Jonah's paranoid interpretation of innocent nostalgia

jokes.text: The country, not the musical.
jokes.setup: Warning that America will become like Greece economically
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he means the struggling nation, not the Broadway show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent deadpan clarification in crisis moment - excellent economy

jokes.text: If we don't put a plan into action tomorrow morning, then by Monday we will be Greece. The country, not the musical.
jokes.setup: Ben warning about economic consequences
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he means economic collapse, not Broadway show
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that adds levity to serious economic warning while maintaining Ben's voice

jokes.text: You can tear them a six-pack of new assholes.
jokes.setup: Gary suggesting Selina yell at Jonah and Dan as stress relief
jokes.punchline: Creative multiplication of the 'tear someone a new asshole' expression
jokes.explanation: Creative expansion of common expression - 'six-pack of new assholes' is memorable

jokes.text: Dan, I cannot fucking believe how terrible you are at your job. What, are you running on a platform of higher taxes and episiotomies?
jokes.setup: Selina berating Dan about campaign performance
jokes.punchline: Combining unpopular tax policy with painful medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Excellent Selina insult combining political and medical knowledge into devastating comparison

jokes.text: What, are you running on a platform of higher taxes and episiotomies?
jokes.setup: Selina berating Dan about the campaign
jokes.punchline: Combining unpopular tax policy with grotesque medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd pairing - horrible political platform combining policy and medical trauma

jokes.text: I'm gonna come up there myself and I'm gonna shoot you in your fucking mouth.
jokes.setup: Selina's escalating rage at Jonah
jokes.punchline: Graphic threat of violence with specific anatomical target
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's complete loss of control, though relies on shock value

jokes.text: You know, Selina Meyer is a second-rate mediocrity whose only achievement is single-handedly tanking the economy. But somehow we're the losers doing anal with each other?
jokes.setup: Jonah responding to Selina's abuse
jokes.punchline: Valid political criticism undermined by fixation on sexual accusations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jonah making valid points but getting distracted by crude details

jokes.text: You know, Selina Meyer is a second-rate mediocrity whose only achievement is single-handedly tanking the economy.
jokes.setup: Jonah's angry response to Selina's call
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal from servile to openly insulting his former boss
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jonah's transformation when he gets confidence

jokes.text: Guess he couldn't get a babysitter. Or could he?
jokes.setup: Jonah delivering his prepared joke about the mayor
jokes.punchline: Double entendre implying sexual misconduct with childcare providers
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's poor judgment and inappropriate humor, relies on sexual misconduct implications

jokes.text: I took the liberty of arranging some hecklers. No charge.
jokes.setup: Audience member yelling at Jonah
jokes.punchline: Bill revealing he planted hostile audience members as free service
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy reveal - Bill's professional sabotage disguised as campaign help

jokes.text: This is so much more beautiful to watch than the birth of my child.
jokes.setup: Bill watching Jonah's campaign destruction
jokes.punchline: Comparing political sabotage favorably to witnessing childbirth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Bill's twisted parental priorities revealed through political revenge

jokes.text: This is so much more beautiful to watch than the birth of my child.
jokes.setup: Watching Jonah's speech disaster unfold
jokes.punchline: Comparing political destruction to his own child's birth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark character moment - Dan's sociopathy comparing career destruction to family joy

jokes.text: That sort of coarse language is far beneath the dignity of this room.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about 'See-you-next-Tuesday-gate'
jokes.punchline: Mike's faux outrage at euphemism while surrounded by actual profanity
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment about selective outrage over language in profanity-filled workplace

jokes.text: She has gone full metal Nixon.
jokes.setup: Discussing Selina's paranoid behavior about the investigation
jokes.punchline: Reference to Watergate-era presidential paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference comparing current paranoia to Nixon's downfall

jokes.text: Gary, C is for cunt.
jokes.setup: Gary confessing to calling Selina a 'crone'
jokes.punchline: Amy having to explain the alphabet to clarify the actual offense
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character moment - treating Gary like a child learning letters

jokes.text: We all called her a cunt.
jokes.setup: The revelation that everyone has used this word about Selina
jokes.punchline: Kent's matter-of-fact summary of universal staff sentiment
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and perfect Kent delivery - clinical summary of workplace hatred

jokes.text: Please don't get me a book for Christmas. I already have a book.
jokes.setup: Selina shopping for Charlie and mentioning books
jokes.punchline: Gary's matter-of-fact admission to owning only one book
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary line - innocent delivery of devastating admission about his literacy habits

jokes.text: I already have a book.
jokes.setup: Selina buying books as Christmas gifts
jokes.punchline: Gary's simple statement revealing he owns exactly one book
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary character moment - simple statement revealing his limited intellectual world

jokes.text: I know you had an eye job.
jokes.setup: Charlie angrily confronting Selina about the bank decision
jokes.punchline: Random accusation about cosmetic surgery in the middle of his rant
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier beauty comments, shows Charlie's hurt feelings

jokes.text: Well, you can thank 'When Gary Met Sally' for that one.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining her decision-making process
jokes.punchline: Combining Gary's romantic influence with rom-com reference
jokes.explanation: Clever movie reference that captures how Gary's romantic nature influenced major decision

jokes.text: Well, you can thank 'When Gary Met Sally' for that one.
jokes.setup: Explaining her decision-making process on the banks
jokes.punchline: Romantic comedy reference with Gary's name substituted
jokes.explanation: Clever movie title pun that captures Gary's sappy influence on major policy decisions

jokes.text: 'Cause this definitely would have killed her.
jokes.setup: Learning about Catherine's relationship with Marjorie
jokes.punchline: Suggesting her conservative mother would have died from shock at lesbian relationship
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor about generational attitudes toward LGBTQ relationships

jokes.text: Pretty much, yeah.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if everybody called her a cunt
jokes.punchline: Amy's matter-of-fact confirmation of universal staff disrespect
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - devastating confirmation delivered with casual understatement

jokes.text: So who called me a cunt? Was it everybody? Pretty much, yeah.
jokes.setup: Selina finally getting results of investigation
jokes.punchline: Amy's matter-of-fact confirmation of universal staff contempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to investigation arc - stark truth delivered with brutal economy

jokes.text: I can't stop smiling. / Neither can I.
jokes.setup: Final scene with Catherine and Marjorie in their relationship
jokes.punchline: Contrast between their happiness and all the chaos around them
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending beat providing contrast to all the political chaos and cynicism
